2606.09475.title: Emergent alignment and the projectability of ethical personas
2606.09475.paper_summary: Work on `emergent misalignment' shows that finetuning LLMs on narrow tasks can induce broadly misaligned behavior. This supports the `persona selection' (PSM) hypothesis: during pre-training, LLMs learn to simulate different characters and perspectives, which can be elicited and refined during post-training. This paper investigates the converse phenomenon, `emergent alignment', and uses it to support and refine the PSM and motivate a novel desideratum for alignment. We finetune a helpful-only model on broad and narrow safety tasks. To create SFT samples, we follow the `Constitutional AI' (CAI) approach and use four constitutions which encode reasonable alignment strategies: deontology, consequentialism, virtue ethics, and aligning AIs as subordinate to human authority. For each of those models, we show that finetuning on two narrow safety sub-categories reliably induces emergent alignment over a representative set of general safety categories, and on safety subcategories that we directly filtered-out of the data sets used for narrow alignment. To test the `PSM' using a more fine-grained evaluation, we used a multidimensional `ethical persona' diagnostic. For each constitutionally finetuned (broad/narrow) model, we evaluate how well their behavior matches their expected signature profile. Our results show that our CAI models acquire their expected ``ethical persona'' -- e.g., the model narrowly fine-tuned on SFT samples created using the consequentialist constitution agrees significantly more with utilitarian than deontological beliefs. Yet our coarse and fine-grained evaluations show that there are significant differences across our (broad/narrow) finetuned CAI models in how well they project. We conclude that alignment strategies should be evaluated, not just on their (in-distribution) general safety performance, but also specifically on their degree of projectability.
2606.09475.paper_summary_zh: 針對「新興不對齊」的研究顯示，對大型語言模型（LLMs）進行狹窄任務的微調可能會引發廣泛的不對齊行為。這支持了「角色選擇」（PSM）假說：在預訓練期間，LLMs 學會模擬不同的角色和觀點，這些角色和觀點可以在後期訓練中被引出和精煉。本文探討了相反的現象「新興對齊」，並利用它來支持和精煉 PSM，並激發對對齊的新期望。我們對一個僅限於幫助的模型進行了廣泛和狹窄安全任務的微調。為了創建 SFT 樣本，我們遵循「憲法人工智慧」（CAI）的方法，使用四部編碼合理對齊策略的憲法：義務論、結果論、美德倫理學，以及將 AI 對齊為服從於人類權威。對於這些模型中的每一個，我們展示了在兩個狹窄安全子類別上進行微調可靠地引發了在一組代表性的一般安全類別上的新興對齊，並且在我們直接從用於狹窄對齊的數據集中過濾掉的安全子類別上。為了使用更細緻的評估來測試「PSM」，我們使用了一個多維的「倫理角色」診斷。對於每個憲法微調的（廣泛/狹窄）模型，我們評估它們的行為與其預期的特徵輪廓的匹配程度。我們的結果顯示，我們的 CAI 模型獲得了其預期的「倫理角色」——例如，對於使用結果論憲法創建的 SFT 樣本進行狹窄微調的模型，與功利主義的信念相比，顯著更同意義務論的信念。然而，我們的粗略和細緻評估顯示，在我們的（廣泛/狹窄）微調 CAI 模型中，投射的效果存在顯著差異。我們得出結論，對齊策略應該被評估，不僅僅是基於它們的（分佈內）一般安全性能，還應特別基於它們的可投射程度。
2606.09475.author: Guillermo Del Pinal et.al.
2606.09475.authors: Guillermo Del Pinal, Youngchan Lee, Cameron McNamara, Alejandro Perez Carballo
